title: FIRST CONTACT — LIFELINE CINEMATIC PASS V2
episodeObjective: Establish first contact with Ethan Hale through lived, real-time movement; guide him to the relay room; make a hard relay/life-support decision; recover Door 12 evidence; end with Hale's memory contradiction and the first intrusive signal.
rulesApplied: Dialogue is written as lived real-time survival, not mission briefing
Choices are short operator commands, never exposition labels
Characters do not say 'I can do A or B' unless the moment naturally forces it
Every visible choice has exactly two options
System messages are kept functional and sparse
Physical movement uses characterBusy and return nodes
Return nodes describe what happened while Hale was gone
Hale is tired, frightened, sarcastic, defensive, and sometimes wrong
Evidence is discovered physically before it becomes lore
Tone target: Lifeline-style interruption, hesitation, motion, and human rhythm

relay_password.title: RELAY ACCESS
relay_password.description: Access code required. Use the active relay authorization, not the deprecated fragment.

power_routing_01.title: RELAY POWER ROUTING
power_routing_01.description: Legacy matching puzzle preserved for compatibility. V5 replaces this with a two-choice relay power decision.

events.text: Please tell me that went somewhere.
My name is Ethan Hale. ECHO Station. Chief engineer.
I'm jammed into a service alcove beside a relay corridor.
Emergency strip on the floor. No ceiling lights.
Something behind the wall keeps ticking.
It stopped when I typed that.
Which currently means 'nearest idiot to a broken panel.'
I used to keep the relay spine and life-support buses from eating each other.
If I'm fake, I'm a fake with extremely committed lung problems.

events.title: BIOMETRIC FRAGMENT: HALE
events.content:
SUBJECT: ETHAN HALE
HEART RATE: ELEVATED
OXYGEN: 32%
SUIT LINK: UNSTABLE
VOICEPRINT MATCH: 81%
events.caption: Partial telemetry fragment transmitted through the relay.

events.text: The suit sounds way too proud of that number.
Then the relay started throwing old voices back at me.
Loops. Distress calls. People who stopped needing help months ago.
You are the first response that feels like a person.
I needed to hear that more than I want to admit.
I'm tired, low on air, and my memory has holes in it.
If I sound too certain, make me prove it.
Cable trays on the left wall.
Some melted clean through their brackets.
My boots are cutting fresh tracks through it.
That's not my favorite discovery today.
Relay room should be past the dark stretch.
I can pull one damaged crew record from the cache.

events.title: CREW LOG: ETHAN HALE
events.content:
DR. ETHAN HALE — CHIEF ENGINEER
RELAY MAINTENANCE AUTHORIZATION: 73_1
STATUS: ACTIVE

NOTE: Secondary maintenance index lists deprecated fragment: _319.
Current authorization must be reconstructed from active record.
events.caption: Recovered identity and maintenance fragment. Authorization appears partially damaged.

events.text: Do not copy the old fragment whole. It is marked deprecated, and stations usually mean that in the least friendly way.
I'm going to the relay door.
Both hands for the corridor. If I keep talking, I'll stop listening.
Took longer than it should have.
Half the corridor is blocked by insulation panels.
The ticking stopped when I passed the third service hatch.
Panel is alive. Keypad is cracked, but the first four digits still respond.
Do not try another random number.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: I can hear the lock cycling inside the frame.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Only one digit fits the damaged spot.
events.speaker: HALE

choices.text: SLOW DOWN. WALK ME THROUGH IT.

events.text: The current code isn't a riddle. It's just damaged.
System says not to use the whole old record.
So we steal the part that fits.
I could also lick the panel and hope it respects confidence.
My memory is not better than evidence right now.
The room is colder than the corridor.
One pulsing like it borrowed a heartbeat and installed it wrong.
Reserve power is still tied to life support.
If I clean the carrier, I have to pull from reserve.
If I leave reserve alone, the signal stays ugly.
I'll force the relay through the dirty line.
Awful is still better than dead.
Just saying the quiet part out loud before the alarms do.
Floor kicks every time the dirty line surges.
Feels like standing on a sleeping animal.
My suit alarm finally stopped scolding me.
There's a security cache behind the second relay column.
I do not remember why looking at it makes my stomach drop.
I can hear the silence between your responses.
That should not feel comforting, but here we are.
Suit alarm hated the power dip.
It made a sound I can only describe as disappointed in me personally.
There's a security cache behind the second relay column.
That memory comes with a bad taste.
Cache is behind the second relay stack.
Relay console is on the other side of the room.
If I leave it alone, the carrier may drift.
The cache panel is under a burned insulation flap.
I can see the edge from here.
Door Twelve is written on the label.
I hate that I recognize my own handwriting.
If the line warbles, don't panic.
Actually, panic quietly. I need both hands.
I was hoping you missed that.
Something is sitting on the edge of the carrier.
Like someone behind you trying not to breathe.
Before it decides what we're allowed to know.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Had to pry the panel with a broken bracket.
There's a sealed evidence packet inside.
My hand went cold when I read it.
Something scratched the inside of the insulation flap.
I don't know how something scratches from inside a wall panel.

events.title: SECURITY LOG
events.content:
21:17 — Door 12 opened.
21:17 — Manual override accepted.
21:18 — Operator identity redacted.
21:20 — Internal sensor flagged decompression risk.
events.caption: Recovered security event from the station archive.

events.title: MEDICAL REPORT
events.content:
21:20 — Casualty reported.
Cause: decompression trauma.
Location: Door 12 access corridor.
Status: fatal.
events.caption: Recovered emergency medical report.

events.title: CAMERA ANALYSIS REPORT
events.content:
SOURCE: DOOR 12 SECURITY FEED
FRAME STATUS: VISUAL DATA CORRUPTED
ANALYSIS RESULT:
- One subject detected outside Door 12.
- Subject biometric outline matches Ethan Hale.
- Object detected in subject's right hand: manual override handle.
- Frame terminated before decompression event.
events.caption: Automated analysis generated from corrupted visual feed. No image recovered.
events.message: [CAMERA ANALYSIS REPORT ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

choices.text: DO YOU REMEMBER ANY OF THIS?

events.text: I can't explain something I don't remember doing.
I know exactly how weak it is.
But if I make up a cleaner answer, we get a nicer lie.
And a worse chance of living through it.
Someone behind me shouting my name.
My hand was near the override.

event.speaker: UNKNOWN
event.text: ASK HIM WHAT HAPPENED AFTER THE DOOR OPENED

events.text: The channel just pulled away from me.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Something pushed into the carrier while we were reading.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: The evidence packet is still open.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: I need to seal it before the cache corrupts.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Operator, if Door Twelve comes up again...
Do not let me step around it.
I don't trust my memory enough to protect either of us.
Curiosity feels like courage until something answers back.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Before it learns anything else from us.
events.speaker: HALE

majorChanges: Rebuilt dialogue to feel like real-time messaging instead of report-style exposition.
Removed awkward lines such as relay corpse, ghost mouth, and one-word fragments.
Added characterBusy events and return nodes for hidden wait-time gameplay.
Converted relay power routing from matching puzzle into a meaningful two-choice survival tradeoff.
Kept the same core scenario: first contact, relay access, Door 12 evidence, Unknown Signal, Episode 02 transition.
Every choice group now has exactly two choices.
Hale reports what happened while he was unavailable instead of returning with empty lines.
Unknown signal appears earlier and reacts differently based on player attention.
